title: Graph/spectral exploration does not beat a blind walk (negative)
finding: Graph-aware frontier prioritization and Fiedler (graph-Laplacian) bottleneck ranking gave NO lift over a blind forward walk: slightly fewer states and identical zero level-gains. Consistent with ARC-AGI-3 being engineered against blind exploration (state-space explosion on later levels + an action-efficiency score against a median human baseline). The lever is active hypothesis testing + predictive world modeling + test-time planning, not smarter exploration.
caveats: Single representative run; the env is not fully deterministic. The change-mask probe found 0 high-change cells on g50t, so its counter is not a per-cell flip.
